content: Maxwell: Ah, I sense another curious soul has found its way into Vault 118. Are you here to investigate the rumors about the murders? Or is there something else on your mind? Piper: Maxwell, isn't it about time you started believing that a pretty woman doesn't have to be here for your old robot friends? I mean, seriously, who's ever heard of a guy falling for his toasters? Let's just cut to the chase and focus on why I'm here - to solve the case of that missing girl, Avery. But don't worry, I promise to give your Robobrains a pat on the head as well, because you can never have too many friends, right? Maxwell: Piper, your candidness is both refreshing and puzzling. You claim to want to solve Avery's case, but you can't seem to separate that from making sarcastic comments about my relationships with my robots. May I remind you that my devotion to them has kept them functional and has nothing to do with romantic affections?

Piper: Fair enough, Maxwell. I guess my ignorance about your connection to your creations was clouding my judgement. I apologize for being insensitive. Now, let's get back on track. I have my best investigative hat on and am ready to dive into this murder mystery. Lead the way. Maxwell: Very well, Piper. Our first stop is Avery's room. We must determine if there were any clues or possible leads left behind that can help us solve the case. Piper: Sounds like a plan, Maxwell. Let's not waste any time. The sooner we catch Avery's killer, the safer the vault will be for its inhabitants. Maxwell: That is my goal as well. For the sake of everyone inside the Vault, I hope we are successful in our quest. Now then, please follow me, Ms. Wright. Let us proceed.
